title: Sack of Constantinople
extract: The sack of Constantinople occurred in April 1204 and marked the culmination of the Fourth Crusade. Crusaders captured and sacked the Byzantine capital, after which the Latin Empire was established.

title: Siege of Masada
extract: The hilltop fortress of Masada, in present-day Israel, was besieged and taken by Roman imperial forces in the final phase of the First Jewish-Roman War. According to Josephus, the siege ended with the mass suicide of the Sicarii rebels and resident Jewish families.

title: Siege of Busanjin
extract: The siege of Busanjin was fought at Busan on 24 May 1592. It was one of the first battles of the Japanese invasions of Korea.

title: Battle of Okpo
extract: The Battle of Okpo was the first naval battle of the Imjin War and the first victory of Admiral Yi Sun-sin against the Japanese naval fleet.

title: Battle of Hansan Island
imageAttribution: War Memorial of Korea Open Archives
extract: At Hansan Island and Angolpo, Yi Sun-sin's fleet destroyed roughly 100 Japanese ships and halted Japanese naval operations along Korea's southern coast.

title: Battle of Haengju
extract: The Battle of Haengju took place on 14 March 1593 during the Japanese invasions of Korea. The Japanese attack failed to overcome Haengjusanseong.

title: Battle of Bibracte
extract: The Battle of Bibracte was fought between a Gallic confederation centred around the Helvetii and a Roman army under Julius Caesar. It was the first major battle of the Gallic Wars.

title: Battle of Vosges
extract: The Battle of Vosges, also referred to as the Battle of Vesontio, was fought in 58 BC between Ariovistus's Germanic coalition and Julius Caesar's Roman army.

title: Battle of the Sabis
extract: The Battle of the Sabis, also known as the Battle of the Sambre, was fought in 57 BC between Julius Caesar's army and Belgic tribes led principally by the Nervii.

title: Battle of Morbihan
extract: The Battle of Morbihan, also known as the Battle of Quiberon Bay, was a decisive Roman naval victory over the Veneti in 56 BC.

title: Battle of Gergovia
extract: The Battle of Gergovia took place in 52 BC at the Arverni stronghold of Gergovia, where Gallic forces under Vercingetorix defeated Julius Caesar's army.

title: Fall of Granada
extract: The Granada War was a series of military campaigns between 1482 and 1492 by Ferdinand II of Aragon and Isabella I of Castile against the Nasrid Emirate of Granada. It ended with Granada's defeat and annexation by Castile, ending the last remnant of Islamic rule on the Iberian peninsula.

title: Siege of the Alcazar
extract: The siege of the Alcázar was a highly symbolic Nationalist victory in Toledo in the opening stages of the Spanish Civil War. The siege ended on September 27, 1936, with the arrival of the Army of Africa under Francisco Franco.

title: Siege of Madrid
imageAttribution: Concern Illustrated Daily Courier - Illustration Archive
extract: The siege of Madrid was a two-and-a-half-year siege of the Republican-controlled Spanish capital by Nationalist armies during the Spanish Civil War. The most intense fighting came during the Nationalist attempt to take the city in November 1936.

title: Battle of Jarama
extract: The Battle of Jarama was an attempt by Nationalist forces to dislodge Republican lines along the Jarama River east of Madrid. After days of fierce fighting, no breakthrough was achieved.

title: Battle of Guadalajara
extract: The Battle of Guadalajara saw the victory of the Spanish Republican Army and International Brigades over Italian and Nationalist forces attempting to encircle Madrid during the Spanish Civil War.

title: Battle of Brunete
extract: The Battle of Brunete was a Republican attempt to relieve pressure on Madrid and the north. The Republicans initially succeeded but were forced to retreat after Nationalist counterattacks.

title: Battle of Teruel
extract: The Battle of Teruel was fought in and around Teruel between December 1937 and February 1938. The city changed hands before being retaken by Nationalist forces.

title: Battle of the Ebro
extract: The Battle of the Ebro was the longest and largest battle of the Spanish Civil War. It was the final major Republican offensive and ended in Nationalist victory.

title: Siege of Detroit
extract: The siege of Detroit, also known as the surrender of Detroit or the Battle of Fort Detroit, was an early engagement in the War of 1812. British forces under Isaac Brock and Indigenous warriors under Tecumseh compelled William Hull to surrender the fort, town, and American army.

title: Burning of Washington
imageAttribution: J. Cundee, publisher; Library of Congress
extract: The Burning of Washington, also known as the Capture of Washington, was a British amphibious attack during the War of 1812. After Bladensburg, British troops entered Washington, D.C., and burned multiple public buildings.

title: Battle of Baltimore
imageAttribution: Unknown artist; original uploader Dr.frog at English Wikipedia
extract: The Battle of Baltimore took place between British and American forces on September 12-14, 1814, during the War of 1812. Baltimore's defense and Fort McHenry's bombardment inspired Francis Scott Key's poem that became the lyrics for the United States national anthem.

title: Battle of Heiligerlee
extract: The Battle of Heiligerlee was the first Dutch victory during the Eighty Years' War, fought between Dutch rebels and the Spanish army of Friesland.

title: Battle of Jemmingen
extract: After Heiligerlee, Louis of Nassau failed to take Groningen and was defeated by the Duke of Alba at Jemmingen on 21 July 1568.

title: Siege of Haarlem
extract: The siege of Haarlem was a bloody Eighty Years' War siege from December 1572 to July 1573. The starving city surrendered and its garrison was massacred.

title: Siege of Leiden
extract: The siege of Leiden occurred during the Eighty Years' War in 1573 and 1574. The siege failed when the city was relieved in October 1574.

title: Battle of Mookerheyde
imageAttribution: Frans van den Wijngaerde, after Johann Wilhelm Baur
extract: At Mookerheyde, Spanish tercios defeated Dutch forces composed of German mercenaries on 14 April 1574 during the Eighty Years' War.

title: Fall of Antwerp
extract: The fall of Antwerp in August 1585 followed a siege lasting over a year and forced the Protestant-dominated city to surrender to Spanish forces under Alessandro Farnese.

title: Battle of Turnhout
extract: The Battle of Turnhout was fought on 24 January 1597 by Dutch and English forces against the Spanish Empire during the Eighty Years' War.

title: Siege of 's-Hertogenbosch
extract: The siege of 's-Hertogenbosch was an action in 1629 during the Eighty Years' War in which a Dutch army captured the city from Spain.

title: Battle of Copenhagen (1801)
extract: In 1801 a British naval fleet defeated Dano-Norwegian ships anchored near Copenhagen, helping break the League of Armed Neutrality.

title: Battle of Copenhagen (1807)
extract: In 1807 British forces bombarded Copenhagen and seized most of the Dano-Norwegian fleet during the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Mystic massacre
imageAttribution: John Underhill; photo-facsimile by Edward Bierstadt
extract: The Mystic massacre, also known as the Battle of Mystic Fort, took place during the Pequot War in 1637.

title: Battle of Bloody Brook
extract: The Battle of Bloody Brook was fought in 1675 between Native forces and the New England Confederation during King Philip's War.

title: Great Swamp Fight
imageAttribution: Ancient and Honorable Artillery Company of Massachusetts
extract: The Great Swamp Fight was a crucial King Philip's War battle between New England militia and the Narragansett people.

title: Battle of Bushy Run
extract: The Battle of Bushy Run was fought in western Pennsylvania during Pontiac's War.

title: Battle of Fallen Timbers
extract: The Battle of Fallen Timbers was the final battle of the Northwest Indian War.

title: Battle of Tippecanoe
extract: The Battle of Tippecanoe was fought in 1811 between U.S. forces and Native forces associated with Tecumseh's Confederacy.

title: Sand Creek massacre
extract: The Sand Creek massacre was an 1864 U.S. Army attack on Cheyenne and Arapaho people in Colorado Territory.

title: Battle of the Washita River
extract: The Battle of the Washita River was an 1868 attack by the 7th U.S. Cavalry on Black Kettle's Southern Cheyenne camp.

title: Battle of the Rosebud
extract: The Battle of the Rosebud was fought in 1876 during the Great Sioux War.

title: Battle of Bear Paw
extract: The Battle of Bear Paw was the final engagement of the Nez Perce War.

title: Wounded Knee Massacre
extract: The Wounded Knee Massacre was the killing of Lakota people by U.S. Army troops at Wounded Knee Creek in 1890.

title: Battle of Yatay
extract: The Battle of Yatay was the first major land battle of the Paraguayan War and an Allied victory over a Paraguayan column near the Uruguay River.

title: Battle of Curupayty
extract: The Battle of Curupayty was a Paraguayan defensive victory in 1866 during the Humaitá campaign of the Paraguayan War.

title: Siege of Humaitá
extract: The Siege of Humaitá was the Allied operation against the Paraguayan fortress complex guarding the Paraguay River.

title: Battle of Lomas Valentinas
extract: The Battle of Lomas Valentinas was an Allied victory in 1868 during the Pikysyry campaign of the Paraguayan War.

title: Battle of Acosta Ñu
extract: The Battle of Acosta Ñu was an 1869 Paraguayan War battle remembered in Paraguay as the Children's Battle.
